title: Entitlement Engine Semantic Contract
description: Source-backed draft semantic contract for the entitlement engine capability, derived from historical source-lineage evidence as lineage. Facets cite verified source-import manifest rows; proof is hermetic/runtime lineage and not fresh USF proof.
aiGuidance: Source-backed draft. Facets cite verified manifest source rows as lineage; proof is hermetic/stale and not current readiness. Do not treat as implementation authority or fresh proof; respect the hexagonal constraint in ADR 0005.

lifecycle.description: Entitlement lifecycle is deny-by-default: an absent or not-granted key denies, an operator grant moves a key to granted, an operator revoke moves it to revoked, and both revoked and absent block access. Tenant self-reads project the catalog of keys as granted, revoked, or not-granted.

stateModel.description: State is an organisation id plus entitlement key with a state of granted or revoked alongside source, metadata, update timestamp, and updater, while the tenant self-read view projects every catalog key into granted, revoked, or not-granted. Feature flags are explicitly distinct from entitlements.

permissions.description: Tenant read requires the tenant entitlements read permission and operator read/write require the platform entitlements read/write permissions, and policy-chain evaluation denies on missing permission before evaluating entitlement. Cross-tenant grants do not leak across the tenant boundary.

contracts.description: The operation surface is a tenant self-read of entitlements, an operator read of a tenant's entitlements, an operator write to grant or revoke, and a service-catalog read. Decisions are enforced through an assert/evaluate seam rather than only returned data.

validation.description: Unknown entitlement keys are rejected without audit, invalid tenant identifiers return 400, feature flags are not treated as entitlements, and cross-tenant grants do not leak. Invalid input changes no state.

errorModel.description: The assertion path throws a typed forbidden error when not entitled, the evaluation path returns a denied decision by permission or entitlement, and invalid tenant or key cases return 400; provider failure returns a typed degraded result fail-closed. Access defaults to denied when state is missing.

auditModel.description: Grant and revoke emit entitlement-granted and entitlement-revoked audit-before-change events, and a failing audit blocks persistence and leaves rows unchanged. Read-side evaluation is not audited as a mutation.

readinessModel.description: Entitlement reads are deterministic once the data substrate is migrated, and degraded mode disables store-dependent mutation while keeping read-only reads and audit visible. Live readiness verifies table existence, forced row-level security, no secret columns, tenant isolation, operator bypass, grant/revoke, and audit-before-change.

proof.description: A unit proof covers the deny-by-default state machine and runtime proofs cover the entitlement seam, permission-before-entitlement ordering, the live data substrate, and the routes, demonstrated in source lineage via hermetic and live-substrate validation. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: No verified UI source path in the manifest yet; author the UI semantic model before generation.
